title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Katchup asks Poirot for assistance with a case at the Bluxton Hall Hotel. Returning to Mrs. Blanche Answorth's apartment, Poirot conducts a methodical session to organize his thoughts, instructing Katchup to write down both known facts and unknown questions.

During their discussion, Poirot analyzes the timing of the murders at the Bruxelles Hotel, noting that the three victims arrived on Wednesday and were killed on the second night. While Katchup suggests the killer might be a meticulous planner concerned with train delays, Poirot posits that the killer could be a woman living in London who already possessed a connection to the victims, rather than a traveler from out of town.

Katchup presents a theory regarding the initials on the murder cufflinks. While Poirot had previously questioned Henry Nigges about the initials "PIJ," Katchup argues that the engraving follows a common habit where the surname is placed in the middle, meaning the initials should be read as "PJI." Although Poirot dismisses the idea of inverting initials as ridiculous, he instructs Katchup to travel to Glebeholme to investigate the "PJI" connection while Poirot remains in London.

On Monday morning, Katchup travels to Glebeholme. After getting lost while searching for the King's Head Inn, Katchup is assisted by a friendly, freckled man wearing glasses. Despite the man's helpfulness, Katchup feels a sense of discomfort with the countryside and the impertinence of the strangers' questions.
